t: They shall be judged […] and they shall not come to the Yahad […]
without [… those who pro]vide the plan[s] and the knowledge of the utt[erly holy …]
light and insigh[t …] the war of the godlike beings in the […]
removing […] Surely the [weap]ons of war[f]ar[e] belong to the God of divine beings [… the armies]
of heaven and the won[ders of all the] divine [spirits] shall run at [His] command, while the voice of tumult [… with]
His might, ar[mies of] divine [spirits] at war in the clouds. But [the victory] shall belong [to the God of divine beings.]
God [by His knowledge has created] wonderful new works. All these has He wondrously created; none can comprehend His glorious plan.
To the King of the [wise] godlike beings belong all matters of knowledge; indeed, the God of knowledge causes all that happens forever. Through His knowledge
and by means of His glorious plan all the eternal seasons have come to be. He has created the former things at their times, and the latter things

at the time appointed for them. None among those who are knowledgeable—those to whom revelation has come—can grasp these things before He does them; even when He brings them into existence, none can truly comprehend them. None of the divine beings
understands what He has designed, for these things are part of His glorious creation, and were [part] of His [plan] before ever they came to be.
